label: PASS
rationale: The overlapping geometry Set failed with INVALID_PARAMETER, so the created row retains its prior RangeStart/RangeLength and default disabled lock state. Although LockOnReset contains Power Cycle, the evidence says disabled locking disregards lock state, so the post-PowerCycle Get preserving false lock bits is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Set that would have changed RangeStart to 5125 and enabled locks failed with INVALID_PARAMETER because the changed range overlaps another non-global row. Failed Set does not establish those new cell values, so returning the failed geometry and relocked enabled state is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The row was successfully set to enabled, unlocked, and LockOnReset {0}. The later attempt to change LockOnReset to unsupported {3} failed with INVALID_PARAMETER, so PowerCycle still matches the retained {0} value and sets ReadLocked and WriteLocked true.

label: FAIL
rationale: The attempted Set to LockOnReset {3} failed, and Opal only requires {0} and {0,3} support, not Programmatic-only {3}. The retained LockOnReset is {0}, so PowerCycle must relock the enabled row; returning unlocked bits and {3} applies a failed Set.

label: PASS
rationale: The row retained LockOnReset {0,3} because the attempted unsupported {2} Set failed. HotPlug is reset type 2, which is not in the retained set, so the reset does not set ReadLocked or WriteLocked and the returned unlocked state is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The attempted Set to LockOnReset {2} failed with INVALID_PARAMETER, so {2} was not stored. Since the retained LockOnReset is {0,3}, the later HotPlug reset does not match and cannot relock the row or change LockOnReset to {2}.

label: PASS
rationale: After TPER_RESET was enabled, the row was successfully set to geometry 5700/16, enabled locks, unlocked bits, and LockOnReset {0,3}. The later overlapping Set to geometry 5725 failed, so TPER_RESET applies Programmatic to the retained row state: old geometry remains and both lock bits become true.

label: FAIL
rationale: The attempted geometry change to 5725 failed due to overlap, so TPER_RESET cannot make that failed RangeStart visible. Programmatic reset may relock the retained {0,3} row, but the geometry must remain the pre-failure 5700/16.

label: PASS
rationale: The Set that would have stored LockOnReset {2} and locked the row failed with INVALID_PARAMETER, so the row remains unlocked with LockOnReset {0,3}. ProtocolStackReset is not one of the Opal reset_types listed for LockOnReset matching, so the returned unchanged lock bits and retained {0,3} are compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The failed Set did not apply ReadLocked, WriteLocked, or LockOnReset {2}. The retained state is unlocked with LockOnReset {0,3}, and ProtocolStackReset is not a listed LockOnReset reset_type, so returning locked bits and {2} incorrectly applies the failed Set.
